title: glechoma micrantha
intro: Glechoma micrantha, also known as Creeping Thyme or Dwarf Thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant with tiny leaves and small purple flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Glechoma micrantha have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of Glechoma micrantha are believed to contribute to various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as skin and digestive problems. Glechoma micrantha can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used as a culinary herb to add flavor to dishes.
